matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: LDLR loss-of-function model (LDLR familial hypercholesterolemia loss-of-function model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an LDLR receptor-mediated-endocytosis thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to LDL particle clearance, LDL-C levels, apoB lipoprotein uptake, familial hypercholesterolemia review, or atherosclerotic lipid biology.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed rs688 dosage suggests this sample carries the LDLR prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests an LDLR receptor-mediated-endocytosis thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to LDL particle clearance, LDL-C levels, apoB lipoprotein uptake, familial hypercholesterolemia review, or atherosclerotic lipid biology. This specific marker is curated as LDLR splicing/lipid association marker. rs688 is curated as a LDLR marker for Familial hypercholesterolemia review context, LDL-C and apoB lipoprotein uptake. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the LDL particle binding, receptor-mediated endocytosis, cholesterol uptake, and familial hypercholesterolemia biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs688 at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the LDLR prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag LDL particle binding, receptor-mediated endocytosis, cholesterol uptake, and familial hypercholesterolemia biology.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an LDLR receptor-mediated-endocytosis thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to LDL particle clearance, LDL-C levels, apoB lipoprotein uptake, familial hypercholesterolemia review, or atherosclerotic lipid biology. An observed LDLR variant places this sample in the curated LDLR research context. The local LDLR bundle is familial-hypercholesterolemia and lipid-uptake oriented. High-impact LDLR findings require clinical-grade classification, inheritance, phenotype, and lipid data. LDLR variants can affect ligand binding, trafficking, recycling, splicing, or receptor abundance.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a LDLR variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests an LDLR receptor-mediated-endocytosis thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to LDL particle clearance, LDL-C levels, apoB lipoprotein uptake, familial hypercholesterolemia review, or atherosclerotic lipid biology. LDLR methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a LDL particle binding, receptor-mediated endocytosis, cholesterol uptake, and familial hypercholesterolemia biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local LDLR bundle is familial-hypercholesterolemia and lipid-uptake oriented. High-impact LDLR findings require clinical-grade classification, inheritance, phenotype, and lipid data. LDLR variants can affect ligand binding, trafficking, recycling, splicing, or receptor abundance.
